info.title: Threa Public API
info.description:
The Threa Public API lets you programmatically read and write messages, list streams, search, and more.

## Authentication

All requests require a Bearer token in the `Authorization` header:

```
Authorization: Bearer thr_your_api_key_here
```

API keys are created by **workspace admins** in the Threa app under **Settings > API Keys**.
Each key is scoped to a workspace and granted specific permissions (scopes).

## Scopes

API keys are granted specific scopes that control access:

- `messages:search` — Grants access to search messages in public streams in a workspace. Application level stream grants can extend permissions to private streams.
- `streams:read` — Grants access to list and search accessible streams in a workspace.
- `messages:read` — Grants access to read messages in accessible streams.
- `messages:write` — Grants access to send, update, and delete messages in accessible streams.
- `users:read` — Grants access to list and search workspace users.
- `memos:read` — Grants access to search preserved workspace memos and inspect their provenance.
- `attachments:read` — Grants access to search accessible attachments, inspect extracted content, and fetch download URLs.
- `bot-runtime:read` — Grants access to read bot runtime presence and invocation state.
- `bot-runtime:write` — Grants access to heartbeat bot runtime presence and link runtime sessions.
- `bot-invocations:read` — Grants access to read bot invocation state.
- `bot-invocations:write` — Grants access to claim, complete, or fail bot invocations.

## Rate Limits

Requests are rate-limited per workspace and per API key. Rate limit headers are included in responses.

## Pagination

List endpoints return paginated results with `hasMore` and `cursor` fields.
Pass the `cursor` value as the `after` query parameter to fetch the next page.

post.description: Full-text and optional semantic search across accessible streams.

metadata.description: External references attached by the sender. Always present; empty when unset.

post.description: Search preserved workspace memos with semantic, exact, or recent-first retrieval.

get.description: Retrieve a memo together with source stream and source message provenance.

post.description: Search accessible attachments by filename or extracted content.

get.description: Retrieve attachment metadata and extracted content for an accessible attachment.
Create a short-lived signed URL for an accessible attachment.

post.summary: Create or link a bot runtime session

sentVia.description: Present when message was sent via API on behalf of a user

metadata.description: External references attached by the sender. Always present; empty when unset.

get.description: List streams accessible to this API key, with optional type and text filters.
Cursor-paginated message list. Use `before` or `after` sequence numbers.

sentVia.description: Present when message was sent via API on behalf of a user

metadata.description: External references attached by the sender. Always present; empty when unset.

post.description: Send a message. Workspace-scoped keys send as a bot; user-scoped keys send on behalf of the key owner.

sentVia.description: Present when message was sent via API on behalf of a user

metadata.description: External references attached by the sender. Always present; empty when unset.

post.description: Find non-deleted messages whose `metadata` contains all the given key/value pairs (AND-containment). Useful for dedup flows — e.g. 'has a message already been posted for this GitHub PR event?'.

sentVia.description: Present when message was sent via API on behalf of a user

metadata.description: External references attached by the sender. Always present; empty when unset.

patch.description: Update a message you previously sent via API.

sentVia.description: Present when message was sent via API on behalf of a user

metadata.description: External references attached by the sender. Always present; empty when unset.

delete.description: Delete a message you previously sent via API.

get.description: List users in the workspace with optional text search and cursor pagination.

Returns a discriminated union describing the authenticated principal — either the API-key owner (`kind: "user"`) or the bot whose key is in use (`kind: "bot"`). Used by clients (e.g. the OpenClaw channel plugin) to verify their key and discover their identity after pairing.
For user-scoped keys: lists the authenticated user's personal bots, optionally filtered by trait. Used by the frontend to enumerate quick-switcher commands. Bot-scoped keys receive 403.

apiKey.description: API key created by a workspace admin in Settings > API Keys. Prefix: `thr_`.

tags.name: Streams

tags.description: Read, send, update, and delete messages
